Solution: Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect

Question: Interfascicular cambium develops from the cells of
Solution: Interfascicular cambium develops from the cells of the medullary rays. The interfascicular cambium is formed from the cells of the medullary rays adjoining the interfascicular cambium.

Question: Which of the following is a basic amino acid?
Solution: Basic part is more than acidic part so lysine is basic amino acid.

Question:
Given below are two statements: One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R:
Assertion A: Late wood has fewer xylary elements with narrow vessels.
Reason R: Cambium is less active in winters.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Options: Both A and R are true, but R is NOT the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A.
Solution: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A

Question: The vascular cambium normally gives rise to
Solution: During secondary growth, vascular cambium gives rise to secondary xylem and secondary phloem. Phelloderm is formed by cork cambium. The periderm is derived from the phellogen, a meristematic region that arises via the dedifferentiation of parenchyma cells in the epidermis, cortex, phloem, or pericycle.

Question: Which of the statements given below is not true about formation of Annual Rings in trees:
Options: Differential activity of cambium causes light and dark bands of tissue-early and late wood respectively
Activity of cambium depends upon variation in climate
Annual rings are not prominent in trees of temperate regions
Annual ring is a combination of spring wood and autumn wood produced in a year
Solution: Growth rings are formed by the differential activity of cambium in different seasons. In plants of temperate regions, cambium is more active during spring and less active in autumn seasons. In temperate regions, climatic conditions are not uniform throughout the year. However, in tropics, climatic conditions are uniform throughout the year.

Question:
In old trees the greater part of secondary xylem is dark brown and resistant to insect attack due to:
(a) Secretion of secondary metabolites and their deposition in the lumen of vessels.
(b) Deposition of organic compounds like tannins and resins in the central layers of the stem.
(c) Deposition of suberin and aromatic substances in the outer layer of the stem.
(d) Deposition of tannins, gum, resin and aromatic substances in the peripheral layers of the stem.
(e) Presence of parenchyma cells, functionally active xylem elements and essential oils.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution: In old trees, the greater part of the secondary xylem is dark brown due to deposition of organic compounds like tannins, resins, oils, gums, aromatic substances and essential oils in the central or innermost layers of the stem. These substances make it hard, durable and resistant to the attacks of microorganisms and insects.

Question: Identify the incorrect statement.
Options: Sapwood is the innermost secondary xylem and is lighter in colour.
Due to deposition of tannins, resins, oils etc., heart wood is dark in colour.
Heart wood does not conduct water but gives mechanical support.
Sapwood is involved in conduction of water and minerals from root to leaf.
Solution: In a large tree, only the outer secondary xylem (sapwood) serves in water conduction, while the inner part (heartwood) is composed of dead but structurally strong secondary xylem and is darker in colour due to deposition of tannins, resins and oils.

Question: The main function of bulliform cells in grasses is :
Options: to make the leaf impermeable to fungal spores.
to minimize water loss during water stress.
Solution: Bulliform cells are large empty colourless cells that lose water and become flaccid in water scarce condition. Hence they curl the leaf inwards to minimise water loss by reducing the exposed surface area.

Question: Specialized epidermal cells surrounding the guard cells are called:
Solution: As subsidiary cells & guard cells, both are the modification of epidermal cells in which guard cells have chloroplasts which are absent in surrounding subsidiary cells. So, the correct answer is 'Subsidiary cells.'

Question: In the given figure, which component has thin outer walls and highly thickened inner walls?
Solution: The outer walls of guard cells (away from the stomatal pore) are thin and the inner walls (towards the stomatal pore) are highly thickened.

Question:
Read the following statements about the vascular bundles
(a) In roots, xylem and phloem in a vascular bundle are arranged in an alternate manner along the different radii.
(b) Conjoint closed vascular bundles do not possess cambium
(c) In open vascular bundles, cambium is present in between xylem and phloem
(d) The vascular bundles of dicotyledonous stem possess endarch protoxylem
(e) In monocotyledonous root, usually there are more than six xylem bundles present
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution:
All the given statements are correct:
• When xylem and phloem within a vascular bundle are arranged in an alternate manner along the different radii, the arrangement is called radial such as in roots.
• Conjoint, closed vascular bundles enclose both xylem and phloem. Xylem is found towards the inner surface and phloem towards the outer surface. Cambium is absent.
• Open vascular bundles are vascular bundles in which secondary growth is possible. This type of vascular bundle has cambium present in between the xylem and phloem.
• Vascular bundles of dicot stem are eight in number, arranged in the form of a broken ring. The vascular bundles are conjoint, collateral and open. Xylem is on the inner surface and phloem on the outer surface. Xylem is described as endarch.
• Vascular bundles in monocot roots are radial, polyarch and exarch. Large number (more than 6) of xylem and phloem groups alternate with each other.

Question:
Read the different components from (a) to (d) in the list given below and tell the correct order of the components with reference to their arrangement from outer side to inner side in a woody dicot stem:
(1) Secondary cortex (2) Wood
(3) Secondary phloem (4) Phellem The correct order is:
Solution: The correct order of the components with reference to their arrangement from outer side to inner side in a woody dicot stem is phellem – secondary cortex – secondary phloem – wood.

Question: Which of the following is made up of dead cells?
Solution: Cork cambium undergoes periclinal division and cuts off thick-walled suberized dead cells toward outside i.e., phellem (cork) and it cuts off thin-walled living cells i.e., phelloderm on the inner side. Parenchyma is living tissue and helps in support and storing food. Collenchyma is also living tissue that provides tensile strength to plants.

Question: Find the statement that is NOT correct with regard to the structure of monocot stem.
Options: Vascular bundles are conjoint and closed
Solution: The monocot stem has a sclerenchymatous hypodermis, a large number of scattered vascular bundles, Vascular bundles are conjoint and closed & phloem parenchyma is absent.

Question: Bulliform cells are responsible for
Options: Protecting the plant from salt stress.
Providing large spaces for storage of sugars.
Inward curling of leaves in monocots.
Solution: In grasses, certain adaxial epidermal cells along the veins modify themselves into large, empty, colourless cells. These are called bulliform cells. When the bulliform cells in the leaves have absorbed water and are turgid, the leaf surface is exposed. When they are flaccid due to water stress, they make the leaves curl inwards to minimise water loss Bulliform cells are therefore responsible for inward curling of leaves in the monocots.

Question:
Identify the correct statements:
A. Lenticels are the lens-shaped openings permitting the exchange of gases.
B. Bark formed early in the season is called hard bark.
C. Bark is a technical term that refers to all tissues exterior to vascular cambium.
D. Bark refers to periderm and secondary phloem.
E. Phellogen is single layered in thickness.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution: A and D only

Question: Casparian strips occur in
Options: <!-StartFragment->Epidermis of both monocot and dicot root<!-EndFragment->
Solution: Endodermis of the root contains a Casparian strip on the radial and inner tangential wall. It is formed by the deposition of suberin. Casparian strips are impermeable for water and it is a characteristic feature of roots.

Question: The balloon- shaped structures called tyloses
Options: Are extensions of xylem parenchyma cells into vessels
Are linked to the ascent to sap through xylem vessels
Originate in the lumen of vessels
Solution: The balloon shaped structures, formed in the vessels of heart wood, due to the extension of parenchyma are called tyloses. They block the vessels. They contain the depositions of bio-chemicals like tannins, resins, gums, etc. Due to the presence of tyloses, heart wood cannot conduct water and heart wood shows resistance to pathogens to insects. So it is highly durable. So, the correct option is ‘are extensions of xylem parenchyma cells into vessels’.

Question: Plant having little or no secondary growth are
Solution:
Secondary growth occurs in dicot and gymnosperms. Grasses are monocots hence they do not perform secondary growth.
Palm like monocots shows anomalous secondary growth.

Question:
Match List-I with List-II :
List-IList-II
A.Conjunctive tissueI.Specialised cells in the vicinity of guard cells
B.Casparian stripsII.Endodermal cells rich in starch
C.Subsidiary cellsIII.Tissue between xylem and phloem
D.Starch sheathIV.Endodermal cells with suberin deposition

Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
Solution:
(A) Conjunctive tissue is the tissue between xylem and phloem.
(B) Casparian strips are found in endodermal cells, they are suberin depositions in the cell wall.
(C) Subsidiary cells are specialised cells in the vicinity of guard cells.
(D) Starch sheath is another name for endodermal cells rich in starch.

Question: Lenticels are involved in
Solution: Lenticels are involved in gaseous exchange between the plant stem and the surrounding.

Question: Cortex is the region found between
Solution:
Cortex is the ground tissue present below the epidermis. It surrounds the central cylinder and endodermis lines the outermost layer of the cylinder. So, the region between epidermis and stele is cortex. Pericycle is present between the endodermis and vascular bundle. The whole ground tissue system (cortex, endodermis and pericycle) is present between epidermis and stele.
Therefore, the correct answer is option d.

Question: Identify the wrong statement in the context of heartwood.
Options: It conducts water and minerals efficiently
It comprises dead elements with highly lignified walls
Organic compounds are deposited in it
Solution: Heartwood is physiologically inactive due to the deposition of organic compounds and tyloses formation, so this will not conduct water and minerals.

Question: Phloem of gymnosperms lacks
Options: Both sieve tubes and companion cells
Solution: Phloem in gymnosperms lacks both sieve tube and companion cells, whereas they contain albuminous cells and sieve cells.

Question:
The transverse section of a plant shows following anatomical features:
(i) Large number of scattered vascular bundles surrounded by bundle sheath.
(ii) Large conspicuous parenchymatous ground tissue.
(iii) Vascular bundles conjoint and closed.
(iv) Phloem parenchyma absent.
Identify the category of plant and its part:
Solution:
All anatomical features mentioned is shown by monocotyledonous stem so  option 
(c) is correct. In monocots vascular bundles are scattered between parenchymatous ground tissue. There is no pith region in monocots. The vascular bundles are closed as they do not have cambium in it.

Question: Grass leaves curl inwards during very dry weather. Select the most appropriate reason from the following:
Options: Shrinkage of air spaces in spongy mesophyll
Solution: Bulliform cells are the modified epidermal cell present in the leaves of grasses. Bulliform cells become flaccid due to water loss. This makes the leaves curl inward to minimise water loss.

Question: Tracheids differ from other tracheary elements in
Solution: Tracheids and vessels together are called tracheary elements as both take part in transport of sap. Tracheids differ from other tracheary elements in being imperforate and their wall is more thickened. The wall of vessels is perforated.

Question: Stomata in grass leaves are
Solution: Monocots contain dumb-bell shaped stomata. Grass being a monocot, has dumb-bell shaped stomata in its leaves. Kidney shaped stomata are present in dicot leaves.

Question: Secondary xylem and phloem in dicot stem are produced by
Solution:
Vascular cambium is partially primary and partially secondary meristem.
It forms the secondary xylem towards the inside and the secondary phloem towards the outsides.
4-10 times more secondary xylem is produced than secondary phloem due to differential activity of cambium.

Question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: Endarch and exarch are the terms often used for describing the position of secondary xylem in the plant body.
Statement II: Exarch condition is the most common feature of the root system.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
Statement I is correct, but Statement II is false.
Statement I is incorrect, but Statement II is true.
Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
Solution: Endarch and exarch are the terms often used for describing the position of primary xylem in the plant body.

Question:
Given below are two statements.
Statement 1: Parenchyma is living but collenchyma is dead tissue
Statement 2: Gymnosperms lack xylem vessels but presence of xylem vessels is the characteristic of angiosperms.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Solution:
Both Parenchyma and Collenchyma are living simple permanent tissue, while sclerenchyma is a dead tissue.
Phloem in Angiosperms is composed of sieve tube elements, companion cells. Gymnosperms have albuminous cells and sieve cells. They lack sieve tubes and companion cells.

Question: You are given a fairly old piece of dicot stem and a dicot root. Which of the following anatomical structures will you use to distinguish between the two?
Solution: In stems, the protoxylem lies towards the centre (pith) and the metaxylem lies towards the periphery of the organ. This type of primary xylem is called endarch. In roots, the protoxylem lies towards the periphery and the metaxylem lies towards the centre. Such arrangement of primary xylem is called exarch.

Question: Age of a tree can be estimated by
Solution: The number of annual rings tells the age of the tree. The number of annual rings suggests the number of years. The annual rings of a tree are made each year when a new layer of wood is added to the trunk and branches of the tree. The study of the rings of trees is called dendrochronology.

Question: Identify the basic amino acid from the following.
Solution: Lysine is the basic amino acid. Valine is neutral amino acid. Tyrosine is an aromatic amino acid. Glutamic acid is acidic amino acid.

Question:
The anatomy of springwood shows some peculiar features. Identify the correct set of statements about springwood.
(a) It is also called early wood.
(b) In the spring season cambium produces xylem elements with narrow vessels.
(c) It is lighter in colour.
(d) The springwood along with autumnwood shows alternate concentric rings forming annual rings.
(e) It has lower density
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution:
In the spring season, cambium is very active. The wood formed during this season is called spring wood or early wood.
- In spring season, cambium produces a large number of xylary elements having vessels
with wider cavities and not narrow cavities.
-The spring wood is lighter in colour.
-The two kinds of woods (spring and autumnwood) appear as alternate concentric rings that constitute an annual ring. Annual rings seen in a cut stem give an estimate of the age of the tree.
-Springwood has a lower density.

Question: Select the correct pair.
Options: Cells of medullary rays that form part of cambial ring - Interfascicular cambium
Loose parenchyma cells rupturing the epidermis and forming a lens-shaped opening in bark - Spongy parenchyma
Large colorless empty cells in the epidermis of grass leaves – Subsidiary cells
In dicot leaves, vascular bundles are surrounded by large thick-walled cells– Conjunctive tissue
Solution: When the cells of medullary rays are differentiated, they give rise to the new cambium called interfascicular cambium. Loose parenchyma cells rupturing the epidermis and forming a lens-shaped opening in the bark are called complementary cells. Large colourless empty cells in the epidermis of grass leaves are called bulliform cells. In the dicot leaf, vascular bundles are surrounded by large thick-walled cells called bundle sheath cells.

Question:
Consider the following features:
(1) Organ system level of organisation
(2) Bilateral symmetry
(3) True coelomates with segmentation on body
Select the correct option of animal groups which possess all the above characteristics:
Solution: True segmentation is present in Annelida, Arthropoda and Chordata. They also have organ system level of organisation, bilateral symmetry and are true coelomates.

Question: Which among these is the correct combination of aquatic mammals?
Solution: Sharks and Trygon (stingray) are the members of Chondrichthyes (cartilaginous fish) while whales, dolphins, and seals are aquatic mammals that belong to class Mammalia.

Question:
Select the incorrect statements from the following:
A. Digestive system in Platyhelminthes is incomplete.
B. Bilateral symmetry is a characteristic feature of adult Echinoderms.
C. Pseudocoelom is possessed by Aschelminthes.
D. Notochord is persistent throughout life in the class Chondrichthyes.
E. Members of class Reptilia maintain a constant body temperature.

Choose the answer from the options given below:
Solution:
$(\mathrm{A}) \rightarrow$ correct → Platyhelminthes have an incomplete digestive system.
(B) → incorrect → Bilateral symmetry is a characteristic feature of larvae of echinoderms. In adult echinoderms, radial symmetry is seen.
(C) → correct → Aschelminthes are characterised by the presence of pseudocoelom.
(D) → correct → Notochord is persistent throughout life in the Chondrichthyes.
(E) → incorrect → Reptiles are cold-blooded organisms and thus, they cannot maintain a constant body temperature.

Warm-blooded organisms like birds and mammals can maintain a constant body temperature.
Thus, as the incorrect statements are indicated by ( B ) and ( E ) only, the correct answer is option (a)

Question: The unique mammalian characteristics are
Options: Pinna, monocondylic skull and mammary glands
Hairs, tympanic membrane and mammary glands
Solution: Hairs, pinna and mammary glands

Question: Infection of Ascaris usually occurs by
Solution: Infection of Ascaris occurs because of the consumption of food and water contaminated with eggs of Ascaris.

Question:
Consider the following statement:
A. Annelids are true coelomates
B. Poriferans are pseudocoelomates
C. Aschelminthes are acoelomates
D. Platyhelminthes are pseudocoelomates
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution:
Poriferans are Acoelomates
Aschelminthes are Pseudocoelomate
Platyhelminthes are Acoelomates

Question:
Match List I with List II.
List IList II
A.Taenia I.Nephridia
B.Paramoecium II.Contractile vacuole
C.Periplaneta III.Flame cells
D.Pheretima IV.Uricose gland

Choose the correct answer from the options give below:
Solution:
List IList II
A.Taenia III.Flame cells
B.Paramoecium II.Contractile vacuole
C.Periplaneta IV.Uricose gland
D.Pheretima I.Nephridia

Question:
Match the following columns and select the correct option.
Column IColumn II
(a) 6-15 pairs of gill slits(i) Trygon
(b) Heterocercal caudal fin(ii) Cyclostomes
(c) Air bladder(iii) Chondrichthyes
(d) Poison sting(iv) Osteichthyes
Options: a – iv; b – ii; c – iii; d – i
a – i; b – iv; c – iii; d – ii
a – ii; b – iii; c – iv; d – i
a – iii; b – iv; c – i; d – ii
Solution:
(a) Cyclostomata that belongs to agnatha which comprises the living jawless
vertebrates.
(b) Chondrichthyes consists of the cartilaginous fishes (eg. shark), a typical member of which has heterocercal (two unequal lobes) caudal fins.
(c) Osteichthyes consists of the bony fishes in which air bladder is present which
regulates buoyancy.
(d) Chondrichthyes poison sting (e.g., Trygon, whose common name is sting ray).

Question: Genus represents
Options: a population of plants and animals
a group of closely related species
a group of closely related families
Solution: Genus comprises a group of related species which has more characters in common in comparison to species of other genera.

Question: Radial symmetry is NOT found in adults of phylum ______.
Solution: Adults of phylum Hemichordata exhibit Bilateral symmetry.

Question: All living members of the class Cyclostomata are:
Solution: All living members of class cyclostomata are ectoparasites.

Question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: When any plane passing through the central axis of the body divides the organism into two identical halves, it is called radial symmetry.
Statement II: In phylum Echinodermata, both adults and larvae are radially symmetrical.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Solution: When any plane passing through the central axis of the body divides the organism into two identical halves, it is called radial symmetry. Adult echinoderms are radially symmetrical while larval echinoderms are bilaterally symmetrical.

Question: Which one of these animals is not a homeotherm?
Solution:
Homeotherm is animals that maintain constant body temperature, irrespective of surrounding temperature. Birds and mammals are homeotherms. Camelus and Macropus are mammals whereas Psittacula is a bird.
Chelone (Turtle) belongs to class reptilia which are Poikilotherm or cold blood.

Question: Which one of the following characteristics is not shared by birds and mammals?
Solution: Both mammals and birds can regulate body temperature and have warm-blooded nature. They have lungs and hence use them for breathing. Both the groups have ossified endoskeletons (formation of bones by adding osteoblasts - osteogenesis, results in an ossified endoskeleton.) But birds lay eggs and show oviparity whereas mammals give birth to live young and show Vivipary (except Monotremes or egg-laying mammals).

Question:
A group of researchers procured some fish like animals and upon investigation the following characters were observed:
A. Endoskeleton was made of cartilage.
B. Ectoparasitic; as they were found attached on fish skin with their circular sucking mouth.
C. Paired fins and scales were absent, but 7 pairs of gill slits were present.

Which of the following species of animals did they consider to fit best with these characters?
Solution:
Petromyzon sp. have cartilaginous endoskeleton. They have circular sucking mouth. They are ectoparasites on some fishes. Their body is devoid of scales and paired fins. They have 7 pairs (6-15 pairs) of gill slits.
Scoliodon and Exocoetus are not parasites.

Question: Which one of the following living organisms completely lacks a cell wall?
Solution: Sea-fan (Gorgonia) belongs to kingdom Animalia and phylum Cnidaria, which completely lacks a cell wall. Saccharomyces is a fungus which has a cell wall majorly composed of chitin. Blue-green algae or cyanobacteria are the eubacteria which are covered by cell wall.

Question:
Select the correct statements with reference to chordates.
A. Presence of mid-dorsal, solid and double nerve cord.
B. Presence of closed circulatory system
C. Presence of paired pharyngeal gill slits
D. Presence of dorsal heart
E. Triploblastic pseudocoelomate animals
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution:
A. Presence of dorsal, hollow nerve cord.
D. Presence of ventral heart
E. Triploblastic coelomate animals

Question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as
Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).
Assertion (A): All vertebrates are chordates but all chordates are not vertebrates
Reason (R): Notochord is replaced by the vertebral column in the adult vertebrates.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
Options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct
Solution: All vertebrates are chordates but all chordates are not vertebrates. This statement is true because all the features of chordates are present in all the vertebrates in some stage of life. In adult vertebrates, the notochord is replaced by the vertebral column and this is not the case for all chordates, hence the assertion is correct and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.

Question:
Following are the statements about the prostomium of earthworms.
(a) It serves as a covering for the mouth
(b) It helps to open cracks in the soil into which it can crawl
(c) It is one of the sensory structures
(d) It is the first body segment
Choose the correct answer from the options given below
Options: (a), (b), (c) and (d) are correct
(b), (c) are correct
(a), (b) and (c) are correct
(a), (b) and (d) are correct
Solution: The anterior end of the earthworm has a mouth that has a covering called prostomium. Prostomium acts as a wedge to force open cracks in the soil. Prostomium has receptors, so it is sensory in function. The first body segment of earthworms is the peristomium.

Question: Select the taxon mentioned that represents both marine and freshwater species.
Solution: Phylum Cnidaria includes both marine and freshwater species. However, most of them are marine, only a few are freshwater species. Ctenophora, Cephalochordata, Echinoderms are found exclusively in the marine environment.

Question: Exoskeleton of arthropods is composed of:
Solution: All arthropods have a hard exoskeleton made of chitin, a type of protein. This shell provides protection for the animals, and gives support for the attachment of the arthropod's muscles. Although arthropods grow, their exoskeletons do not grow with them.

Question: Which of the following characteristic features always holds true for the corresponding group of animals?
Options: Possess a mouth with an upper and a lower jaw – Chordata
Solution: Chondrichthyes is a class that contains the cartilaginous fishes: they are jawed vertebrates with paired fins, paired nares, scales, a heart with its chambers in series, and skeletons made of cartilage rather than bone. Viviparous - Giving birth to living young that develop within the mother's body rather than hatching from eggs. Not all mammals are viviparous like platypus. All chordates do not possess a mouth with an upper and lower jaw. All reptiles have three-chambered hearts, except crocodiles, which have four-chambered hearts (2 atria, 2 ventricles), like mammals and birds.

Question: Select the set of fishes which belong to the class Osteichthyes:
Options: Flying fish, Angel fish and Fighting fish
Saw fish, Fighting fish and Dog fish
Solution:
The correct answer is option (3).
Flying fish (Exocoetus) is a marine bony fish. Angel fish (Pterophyllum) and fighting fish (Betta) are aquarium bony fishes.
Option (1) is incorrect as:-
Devil fish (Octopus) and cuttlefish (Sepia) are molluscs.
Hag fish (Myxine) is a cyclostome.
Option (4) is incorrect as:-
Saw fish (Pristis) and dog fish (Scoliodon) are cartilaginous fishes.
Option (2) is incorrect as:-
Starfish (Asterias) is an echinoderm.

Question: Which of the following features is not present in the phylum Arthropoda?
Solution: Arthropods have a rigid chitinous exoskeleton that covers their complete body. They are characterized by a segmented body with repetitions of organs and tissues at intervals i.e. metameric segmentation and have paired, jointed appendages to perform different functions. Parapodia are lateral appendages of annelids and are characteristic features of Nereis (Phylum Annelida), not Arthropoda.

Question: Planaria possess high capacity of:
Solution: Planaria is a flatworm which possesses a high capacity of regeneration. They have a high capacity of the generation due to the neoplastic cells which are found in them. If an individual is cut transversely into two parts, the anterior segment will generate a new tail and posterior end will develop a new head.

Question:
Read the following statements.
(a) Metagenesis is observed in Helminths.
(b) Echinoderms are triploblastic and coelomate animals.
(c) Roundworms have organ-system level of body organization.
(d) Comb plates present in ctenophores help in digestion.
(e) Water vascular system is characteristic of Echinoderms.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
Options: (a), (d) and (e) are correct
(b), (c) and (e) are correct
(c), (d) and (e) are correct
(a), (b) and (c) are correct
Solution: Metagenesis is the characteristic of cnidarians. Those cnidarians which exist in both forms exhibit alternation of generation (Metagenesis), i.e., polyps produce medusae asexually and medusae form the polyps sexually (e.g., Obelia). Ctenophores, commonly known as sea walnuts or comb jellies are exclusively marine, radially symmetrical, diploblastic organisms with tissue level of organization. The body bears eight external rows of ciliated comb plates, which help in locomotion.

Question: Which of the following is not a characteristic of chordates?
Solution: The members of phylum Chordata show following features: (1) Presence of notochord (2) Pharynx perforated by gill slits (3) Dorsal, hollow and single central nervous system (4) Ventral heart (5) A post-anal tail. Gill slits are absent in non-chordates.

Question:
Match List I with List II:
List IList II
A. PterophyllumI. Hag fish
B. MyxineII. Saw fish
C. PristisIII. Angel fish
D. ExocoetusIV. Flying fish

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution:
Pterophyllum - Angel fish
Myxine - Hag fish
Pristis - saw fish
Exocoetus - Flying fish

Question: Which group of animals belongs to the same phylum?
Solution:
Prawns, scorpions and locusts belong to the phylum Arthropoda. Hence, the (c) option is correct.

Whereas malarial parasite is a sporozoan, Amoeba is an amoeboid protozoan and mosquito belongs to Arthropoda. Hence, the (a) option is wrong.

Earthworm belongs to Annelida, Pinworm belongs to Ascheminthes, Tapeworm belongs to Platyhelminthes. Hence, the (b) option is incorrect.

Sponge belongs to Porifera, Sea anemone belongs to Cnidaria, Starﬁsh belongs to Echinodermata. Hence, the (d) option is also incorrect.

Question: An important characteristic that Hemichordates share with Chordates is
Solution: Pharyngeal gill slits are present in hemichordates as well as in chordates. The notochord is present in chordates only. The ventral tubular nerve cord is a characteristic feature of non-chordates.

Question: Which one of the following organisms bears hollow and pneumatic long bones?
Solution: The scientific name of Vulture is Neophron. Vultures belong to the class Aves. Aves possess an endoskeleton that is fully ossified (bony) and the long bones are hollow with air cavities (pneumatic). Macropus is the scientific name of kangaroo. Ornithorhynchus is the scientific name of Platypus. They both belong to class Mammalia. Hemidactylus is the scientific name of a Wall lizard that belongs to class reptilia.

Question: Which one of the following belongs to the family Muscidae?
Solution: Housefly belongs to the family Muscidae. Fireflies are placed in the family Lampyridae of class Insecta. The grasshopper is also an insect placed in the family Acrididae. A cockroach is also an insect placed in the family Blattidae.

Question: Which is the National Aquatic Animal of India?
Solution: River Dolphin is the National Aquatic Animal of India. This mammal is also said to represent the purity of the holy Ganga as it can only survive in pure and fresh water.

Question: In which of the following animals, does the digestive tract have additional chambers like crop and gizzard?
Solution: Crop and gizzard are part of the digestive tract of birds (class Aves) and insects (phylum Arthropoda). Pavo, Psittacula, Corvus are birds hence all three have crops and gizzard. Chameleon, Bangarus and Crocodilus are reptiles and reptiles do not have crop and gizzard in their digestive system. Bufo belongs to class Amphibia and Balaenoptera is a mammal.

Question:
Match the following organisms with their respective characteristics:
Column-IColumn-II
APila(i)Flame cells
BBombyx(ii)Comb plates
CPleurobrachia(iii)Radula
DTaenia(iv)Malpighian tubules
Solution:
(a) Pila is a Mollusc. The mouth contains a file- like a rasping organ for feeding called radula.
(b) Bombyx is an Arthropod. In Bombyx excretion takes place through malpighian tubules.
(c) Pleurobrachia is Ctenophore. The body bears eight external rows of ciliated comb plates, which help in locomotion.
(d) Taenia is a platyhelminth. Specialised cells called flame cells help in osmoregulation and excretion in it.

Question:
Which of the following statements are true for the phylum Chordata?
(i) In Urochordata, notochord extends from head to tail and it is present throughout their life.
(ii) In Vertebrata, the notochord is present during the embryonic period only.
(iii) Central nervous system is dorsal and hollow.
(iv) Chordata is divided into 3 subphyla: Hemichordata, Tunicata, and Cephalochordata.
Solution:
(1) In Urochordata, notochord is present only in larval tail, while in Cephalochordata, it extends from head to tail region and is persistent throughout their life.
(2) Vertebrates possess notochord during the embryonic period. The notochord is replaced by a cartilaginous or bony vertebral column in the adult.
(3) Central nervous system is dorsal and hollow.
(4) Chordata is divided into 3 subphyla: Urochordata, Cephalochordata and Vertebrata.
S.NoChordatesNon-Chordates
1.Notochord presentNotochord absent.
2.Central nervous system is dorsal, hollow and single.The central nervous system is ventral, solid and double
3.Pharynx perforated by gill slits.Gill slits are absent.
4.Heart is ventral.Heart is dorsal (if present)
5.A post-anal part (tail) is present. Post-anal tail is absent.

Question:
Role of the water vascular system in Echinoderms is:
A. Respiration and Locomotion
B. Excretion and Locomotion
C. Capture and transport of food
D. Digestion and Respiration
E. Digestion and Excretion
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution: Water vascular system is involved in respiration, locomotion, capture of prey and transport of food.

Question: A jawless fish, which lays eggs in fresh water and whose ammocoetes larvae after metamorphosis return to the ocean is:
Solution: Petromyzon is marine but migrates for spawning to fresh water. After spawning, within a few days, the fish die. Their larvae, after metamorphosis, return to the ocean.

Question:
The following are the statements about non-chordates :
A. Pharynx. is perforated by gill slits.
B. Notochord is absent.
C. Central nervous system is dorsal.
D. Heart is dorsal if present.
E. Post anal tail is absent.
Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
Solution:
Non-Chordates
Notochord is absent.
Central nervous system is ventral, solid and double.
Gill slits are absent.
Heart is dorsal (if present).
Post-anal tail is absent.

Question: Bilaterally symmetrical and acoelomate animals are exemplified by
Solution: Flatworms are bilaterally symmetrical, triploblastic and acoelomate animals with organ level of organisation. Aschelminthes are triploblastic, bilaterally symmetrical and pseudocoelomate. Annelida are triploblastic, bilaterally symmetrical and coelomate.  Ctenophora is   radially symmetrical, diploblastic.

Question: In case of poriferans, the spongocoel is linked with flagellated cells called
Solution: Spongocoel is a large central cavity in the sponges. Water enters through the numerous ostium in the cavity and exits through a central pore known as an osculum. Choanocytes are the flagellated cells which line the spongocoel. It helps to set up water current and helps in intake of food.

Question: Metagenesis refers to:
Options: Alternation of generation between asexual and sexual phases of an organism
Occurrence of a drastic change in form during post-embryonic development
Presence of a segmented body and parthenogenetic mode of reproduction
Solution: Metagenesis refers to alternation of generation between asexual and sexual phases of an organism within the same species.

Question: The flightless bird with forelimbs modified as paddle-like structures suited for swimming is known as:
Solution:
• Neophron is vulture and Psittacula is a parrot. Both perform flight.
- Struthio is ostrich and Aptenodytes is penguin. Both are flightless birds.
- In penguins, forelimbs are modified into flippers (paddle like structure) and are used for swimming. In ostriches, forelimbs are small and used for balance while running, not for swimming.

Question: Which of the following animals does not undergo metamorphosis?
Solution:
Metamorphosis refers to the transformation of a larva into an adult.
Animals that perform metamorphosis are said to have indirect development.
In earthworm, development is direct which means no larval stage and hence no metamorphosis is present.

Question: Which of the following are correctly matched with respect to their taxonomic classiﬁcation?
Options: Flying ﬁsh, cuttleﬁsh, silverﬁsh, scorpion - Insecta
House ﬂy, butterﬂy, tsetse fly, silverﬁsh – Insecta
Spiny anteater, sea urchin, sea cucumber - Echinodermata
Solution: Houseﬂy, butterﬂy, tsetse ﬂy and silverﬁsh are members of Class Insecta belonging to the phylum Arthropoda.

Question: One of the representatives of phylum Arthropoda is
Solution: Cuttlefish belongs to Phylum Mollusca. Silverfish belongs to Phylum Arthropoda. It is a small, primitive, wingless insect, not a fish. Pufferfish and flying fish belong to Phylum Chordata.

Question: Among the following edible fishes, which one is a marine fish having a rich source of omega-3 fatty acids?
Solution:
Oily fishes store omega-3 fatty acids (oil) in their tissues and body cavity and are the richest source of omega-3 fatty acid. Fish oils contain the omega-3 fatty acids eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) and docosahexaenoic acid (DHA). The most widely available dietary source of EPA and DHA is oily fish, such as salmon, herring, mackerel, anchovies, menhaden, and sardines. Among the given options, Mackerel is oily fish and is the rich source of oil.
Therefore, the correct answer is option c.

Tags.Subject: Biology
Tags.Chapter: Strategies for enhancement in food production

Tags.Subject: Botany
Tags.Chapter: Strategies for enhancement in food production

Question: Which of the following features is not present in Periplaneta Americana?
Options: Indeterminate and radial cleavage during embryonic development
Solution: Indeterminate and radial cleavage is a feature of deuterostomes while cockroach is an example of protostomes. Protostomes develop the mouth before the anus, they also have spiral and determinate cleavage. Spiral cleavage refers to the rotational pattern of the dividing cells, and determinate means that each cell has a certain fate - they cannot become whole organisms if isolated from other cells.

Question: Identify the vertebrate group of animals characterized by crop and gizzard in its digestive system
Solution:
The digestive tract of Aves has additional chambers in their digestive system as crop and Gizzard.
The crop is concerned with the storage of food grains.
The gizzard is a masticatory organ in birds used to crush food again.

Question: Body having meshwork of cells, internal cavities lined with food filtering flagellated cells and indirect development are the characteristics of phylum
Solution: In Porifera, water enters through minute pores (ostia) in the body wall into a central cavity, spongocoel, from where it goes out through the osculum. This pathway of water transport is helpful in food gathering, respiratory exchange and removal of waste. Fertilisation is internal and development is indirect, having a larval stage which is morphologically distinct from the adult.

Question: Choose the CORRECT statement.
Options: All cyclostomes do not possess jaws and paired fins
All reptiles have a three-chambered heart
All Pisces have gills covered by an operculum
Solution:
Cyclostomata is the historical class of jawless fishes that lack paired fins without any exception. All reptiles have three-chambered hearts but the exception is Crocodiles have four-chambered heart. Class Chondrichthyes are cartilaginous fishes that lack operculum. Monotremes are the egg laying mammals.
Therefore, the correct answer is option A.

Question:
Match the following columns and select the correct option –
Column IColumn II
(a) Gregarious, polyphagous pest(i) Asterias
(b) Adult with radial symmetry and larva with bilateral symmetry(ii) Scorpion
(c) Book lungs(iii)Ctenoplana
(d) Bioluminescence(iv) Locusta
Options: a – iii; b – ii; c – i; d – iv
a – ii; b – i; c – iii; d – iv
a – i; b – iii; c – ii; d – iv
a – iv; b – i; c – ii; d – iii
Solution:
(a) Locusta is a gregarious pest.
(b) In Echinodermata, adults are radially symmetrical and larva are bilaterally symmetrical.
For example: Asterias.
(c) In Arthropoda, scorpion respires through book lungs.
(d) Bioluminescence (the property of a living organism to emit light) is well marked in ctenophores. For example: Ctenoplana.

Question:
Find out the correct option after matching the name of the animal (Column I) with one characteristic (Column II) and the phylum/class (Column III) to which it belongs.
Column IColumn IIColumn III
(1)PetromyzonEctoparasiteCyclostomata
(2)IchthyophisTerrestrialReptilia
(3)LimulusBody covered by chitinous exoskeletonPisces
(4)AdamsiaRadially symmetricalPorifera
Options: Limulus - Body covered by chitinous exoskeleton - Pisces
Solution: Petromyzon is a vertebrate belonging to Cyclostomata which is an ectoparasite on marine ﬁsh and turtles. Ichthyophis is a limbless amphibian. Limulus (king crab) is a living fossil, which belongs to the phylum Arthropoda. Admasia having polyp body form is a Coelenterata.

Question:
Match List I with List II:
List IList II
A. PleurobrachiaI. Mollusca
B. RadulaII. Ctenophora
C. StomochordIII. Osteichthyes
D. Air bladderIV. Hemichordata

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution:
(a) A-II, B-I, C-IV, D-III
Pleurobrachia - Ctenophora
Radula - Mollusca
Stomochord - hemichordata
Air bladder - Osteichthyes

Question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R).
Assertion (A): All vertebrates are chordates but all chordates are not vertebrate.
Reason (R): The members of subphylum vertebrata possess notochord during the embryonic period, the notochord is replaced by a cartilaginous or bony vertebral column in adults.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is not the correct explanation of A.
Solution: All vertebrates are chordates but all chordates are not vertebrates. This statement is true because all the features of chordates are present in all the vertebrates in some stage of life. In adult vertebrates, the notochord is replaced by the vertebral column and this is not the case for all chordates, hence the assertion is correct and reason is the correct explanation of assertion.

Question: The chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods is formed by the polymerisation of:
Solution: The chitinous exoskeleton of arthropods is formed by the polymerisation of N-acetyl glucosamine, which is a derivative of glucose.

Question: Which one of the following animals has two separate circulatory pathways?
Solution: Double circulatory system is a characteristic feature of mammals. Whale is a mammal hence, it has two separate circulatory pathways for blood flow.

Question: A marine cartilaginous fish that can produce electric current is
Solution: Torpedo also called electric ray can produce electric current. They possess a pair of electric organs, modified muscle tissues. They produce electric discharge for communication, defense and navigation.

Question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: The class name Reptilia refers to creeping or crawling mode of locomotion.
Statement II: All organisms belonging to Reptilia have three chambered heart.
In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
Options: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Solution: The class Reptilia refers to their creeping or crawling mode of locomotion. Heart is usually three-chambered, but four-chambered in crocodiles. So, All organisms belonging to class Reptilia does not possess three chambered heart.

Question: While trying to find out the characteristic of a newly found animal, a researcher did the histology of adult animal and observed a cavity with presence of mesodermal tissue towards the body wall but no mesodermal tissue was observed towards the alimentary canal. What could be the possible coelom of that animal?
Solution: The above explanation defines pseudocoelom condition

Question:
Arrange the following in descending order of number of species in the Amazonian rain forest.
(a) Plants
(b) Birds
(c) Fishes
(d) Invertebrates
(e) Mammals
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
Solution: The Amazonian rain forest is home to more than 40,000 species of plants, 3,000 of fishes, 1300 of birds, 427 of mammals, 378 of reptiles and of more than 1,25,000 invertebrates. Hence the correct decreasing order would be (d) > (a) > (c) > (b) > (e).

Question:
Match List I with List II.
List IList II
A. Robert MayI. Species-Area relationship
B. Alexander von HumboldtII. Long term ecosystem experiment using out door plots
C. Paul EhrlichIII. Global species diversity at about 7 million
D. David TilmanIV. Rivet popper hypothesis

Choose the correct answer from the options given below.
Solution:
Robert MayGlobal species diversity at about 7 million
Alexander von HumboldtSpecies-Area relationship
Paul EhrlichRivet popper hypothesis
David TilmanLong term ecosystem experiment using out door plots

Question: Red List contains data or information on
Options: Plants whose products are in international trade
Solution:
Red list of IUCN is a step to conserve biodiversity and it includes names of all threatened species. Threatened species are the species which are prone to be endangered in near future. For example, red pandas.
Therefore, the correct answer is option b.

Question: A species facing extremely high risk of extinction in the immediate future is called
Solution: A species facing extremely high risk of extinction in the immediate future is called critically endangered species. A species that has no living members is called as extinct species. Endemic species are plant and animal species that are found in a particular geographical region and nowhere else in the world. A species is vulnerable when it is not critically endangered or endangered but is facing a high risk of extinction in the medium-term future.

Question:
These are regarded as major causes of biodiversity loss:
A. Over exploitation
B. Co-extinction
C. Mutation
D. Habitat loss and fragmentation
E. Migration
Choose the correct option:
Options: A, B, C and D only
Solution:
Causes of biodiversity losses: The accelerated rates of species extinctions that the world is facing now are largely due to human activities. There are four major causes:
• Habitat loss and fragmentation
• Over-exploitation
• Alien species invasions
• Co-extinctions

Question: Among ‘The Evil Quartet’, which one is considered the most important cause driving extinction of species?
Solution: Habitat loss and fragmentation is the most important cause driving animals and plants to extinction.

Question: Which of the following is an in situ conservation method?
Solution: In-situ conservation is exemplified by sacred groves. Wildlife Safari parks, Botanical gardens, seed banks are examples of in-situ conservation.

Question: Which of the following is not a method of ex situ conservation?
Solution: National parks are a method of in situ conservation of biodiversity where organisms are protected within their natural habitat.

Question: Which one of the following is related to Ex-situ conservation of threatened animals and plants?
Solution: Ex-situ conservation is an offsite strategy for the conservation of animals and plants in the zoological parks and botanical gardens. In this method, the endangered species are brought out of their habitats and protected in artificial habitats. In the given question, wildlife safari parks is an example of ex-situ conservation as it protects animals and plants in an artificial environment. The rest of the options are in situ conservation.

Question: All of the following are included in ‘Ex-situ conservation’ except
Solution: Sacred groves are an example of in-situ conservation. Sacred groves are forest fragments, which are protected by religious communities and have a significant religious connotation for the protecting community. Sacred groves are mini forests with rich diversity.

Question: Use of bioresources by multinational companies and organisations without authorisation from the concerned country and its people is called
Solution: Biopiracy term refers to the use of bioresources by multinational companies and other organizations without proper authorization and compensation from the countries or to the indigenous people themselves.

Question: A ‘new’ variety of rice was patented by a foreign company, though such varieties have been present in India for a long time. This is related to
Solution:
In 1997, an American company got patent rights on Basmati rice through the US patent and trademark office that had actually been derived from Indian farmers’ varieties.
The diversity of rice in India is one of the richest in the world, 27 documented varieties of Basmati are grown in India.
Indian basmati rice was crossed with semi-dwarf varieties and claimed as an invention or a novelty.
Sharbati Sonora and Lerma Rojo are varieties of wheat.

Co-667 is a variety of soyabean.

Question: A phosphoglyceride is always made up of
Options: Only a saturated fatty acid esteriﬁed to a glycerol molecule to which a phosphate group is also attached
Only an unsaturated fatty acid esteriﬁed to a glycerol molecule to which a phosphate group is also attached
A saturated or unsaturated fatty acid esteriﬁed to a glycerol molecule to which a phosphate group is also attached
A saturated or unsaturated fatty acid esteriﬁed to a phosphate group which is also attached to a glycerol molecule
Solution: A phosphoglyceride molecule consists of a saturated or unsaturated fatty acid esteriﬁed to a glycerol molecule to which a phosphate group is also attached.

Question: The Earth Summit held in Rio de Janeiro in 1992 was called
Options: For conservation of biodiversity and sustainable utilization of its benefits
To assess threats posed to native species by invasive weed species
For immediate steps to discontinue use of CFCs that were damaging the ozone layer
To reduce CO<sub>2</sub> emissions and global warming
Solution: The United Nations Conference on Environment and Development, also known as Earth Summit (Rio Summit) held in 1992, called upon all nations to take appropriate measures for conservation of biodiversity and sustainable utilisation of its benefits.

Question: Joint Forest Management Concept was introduced in India during
Solution: Joint forest management or JFM is a programme launched by the Government of India in the 1980s. It aims at establishing a partnership between local communities and state forest departments. It is practiced in 10.25 million hectares of degraded forests through 36,075 village forest protection committees. It is useful to local and tribal people as they become partners in the conservation of forests.

Question: According to Robert May, the global species diversity is about
Solution:
Robert May was a theoretical ecologist who established the field of theoretical ecology and population biology. According to him, the global species diversity is about 
7 million.

Question:
The region of Biosphere Reserve which is legally protected and where no human
activity is allowed is known as
Solution:
Biosphere reserve is a protected area with multipurpose activities. It has three zones
(a) Core zone - without any human interference
(b) Buffer zone - with limited human activity
(c) Transition zone - human settlement, grazing cultivation, etc., are allowed.

Question: "The Evil Quartet" of biodiversity loss includes which of the following?
Options: Over-exploitation; Alien species invasions; Air pollution; Co-extinctions
Habitat loss and fragmentation; over-exploitation; Alien species invasions; Co-extinctions
Habitat loss and fragmentation; Air pollution; Water pollution; Co-extinctions
Over-exploitation; Alien species invasions; Soil pollution; Co-extinctions
Solution: 'The Evil Quartet' is the Sobriquet used to describe the four major causes of biodiversity loss which includes Habitat loss and fragmentation, over-exploitation; Alien species invasions and Co-extinctions.

Question: Which of the following is the most important cause for animals and plants being driven to extinction?
Solution:
Habitat loss and fragmentation is the most important cause of driving animals and plants to extinction.
eg: Loss of tropical rainforest reducing the forest cover from 14% to 6%.

Question: An example of ex situ conservation is:
Solution: Ex-situ conservation is the preservation of samples of biological origin outside their natural habitats. This involves conservation of genetic resources as well as wild and cultivated species. A seed bank stores seeds as a source for planting in case seed reserves elsewhere are destroyed. It is a type of gene bank. The seeds stored may be food crops or those of rare species to protect biodiversity. Whereas in-situ conservation is the conservation and maintenance of samples of living organisms in their natural habitat. Wildlife sanctuary, sacred groves and national parks are examples of in-situ conservation.

Question: Which of the following is the most important cause of animals and plants being driven to extinction?
Solution: The accelerated rates of species extinction are largely due to human activities. The major cause of biodiversity loss is habitat loss and fragmentation. The most dramatic example of habitat loss is the tropical rainforests. Once covering more than 14% of earth's land surface, these rainforests now don't cover even 6% of the land surface.

Question: In situ conservation refers to:
Options: Protect and conserve the whole ecosystem
Solution: In situ conservation of biodiversity attempts to conserve organisms at their natural habitat. It not only is aimed at protecting the organisms themselves, but also the ecosystem where they live.

Question: Which one of the following is not a method of in situ conservation of biodiversity?
Solution:
The botanical garden comes under ex-situ conservation (off-site conservation) i.e. living plants (flora) are conserved in artificial human-managed systems.

While others are examples of in-situ conservation methods.

Question:
Tropical regions show greatest level of species richness because
A. Tropical latitudes have remained relatively undisturbed for millions of years, hence more time was available for specks diversification.
B. Tropical environments are more seasonal.
C. More solar energy is available in tropics
D. Constant environments promote niche specialization.
E. Tropical environments are constant and predictable.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Options: A, C, D and E only
Solution: Tropical environments, unlike temperate ones, are less seasonal, relatively more constant and predictable.

Question:
Match List I with List II :
List IList II
A.The Evil QuartetI.Cryopreservation
B.Ex situ conservationII.Alien species invasion
C.Lantana camaraIII.Causes of biodiversity losses
D.DodoIV.Extinction

Choose the option will all correct matches.
Solution: The correct answer is option (4): A–III, B–I, C–II, D–IV. The Evil Quartet refers to the four major causes of biodiversity loss—habitat loss, overexploitation, alien species invasion, and co-extinction—hence matches with III. Ex situ conservation involves preserving biodiversity outside natural habitats through methods like cryopreservation, matching with I. Lantana camara is an invasive alien species that disrupts native ecosystems, so it matches with II. The Dodo is a well-known extinct species, representing extinction, and matches with IV.

Question: How many hotspots of biodiversity in the world have been identified till date by Norman Myers?
Solution:
The concept of biodiversity hotspot was given by Myers who first recognized 10 hotspots that have high biodiversity. A hotspot is a natural habitat that has at least more than 0.5% of total vascular plants as endemic species and is under threat by human activities. Total 34 hotspots have been identified till date that cover only 2.3% of earth surface but are habitat for 77% of the world's species.
Therefore, the correct answer is option b.

Question:
Consider the following statements:
(a) Coenzyme or metal ion that is tightly bound to enzyme protein is called prosthetic group.

(b) A complete catalytic active enzyme with its bound prosthetic group is called apoenzyme.
Solution: Coenzyme or metal ion that is tightly bound to enzyme protein is called prosthetic group. A complete catalytic active enzyme with its bound prosthetic group is called holoenzyme

Question: List of endangered species was released by
Solution:
The IUCN Red List (2004) documents the extinction of 784 species (including 338 vertebrates, 359 invertebrates and 87 plants) in the last 500 years.
Established in 1964, The International Union for Conservation of Nature's Red List of Threatened Species has evolved to become the world's most comprehensive information source on the global conservation status of animal, fungi and plant species.

Question: Which one of the following is not used in ex situ plant conservation?
Solution: Shifting cultivation is a type of deforestation and not a conservation strategy.

Question: Which of the following National Parks is home to the famous musk deer or hangul?
Options: Dachigam National Park, Jammu and Kashmir
Solution:
Bandhavgarh National Park, Madhya Pradesh is famous for Bengal tiger.
Eaglenest or Eagle's Nest Wildlife Sanctuary is famous for its birds and tiger.
Dachigam National Park, Jammu and Kashmir is famous for Hangul, Kashmir stag.
Keibul Lamjao National Park, Manipur is famous for brow-antlered deer and flagship species. Therefore, the correct answer is option c.

Question: Given below is the representation of the extent of global diversity of invertebrates. What groups do the four portions (A-D) represent respectively?
Options: A – Crustaceans, B – Insects, C – Molluscs, D - Other animal group
A - Molluscs, B - Other animal group, C - Crustaceans, D - Insects
A - Insects, B - Molluscs, C - Crustaceans, D - Other animal group
A – Insects, B - Crustaceans, C - Other animal group, D - Molluscs
Solution: Phylum-Arthropoda is the largest phylum of the animal kingdom with its largest group, i.e. Insecta (A). The second largest population is of phylum Mollusca (B). The third one is occupied by crustaceans (C). All other animals together indicate the (D) portion of the pie chart.

Question: The species confined to a particular region and not found elsewhere is termed as
Solution: The species confined to a particular region and not found elsewhere is termed as endemic species.

Question: Which one of the following is an example of ex-situ conservation?
Solution: Ex-situ is off site conservation i.e. conservation of living organisms out of their natural habitat. Zoos and Botanical Garden are examples of the same.

Question: Which of the following is CORRECTLY matched?
Solution:
Parthenium hysterophorus is a common invasive weed and causes damage to the wheat crop. Its infection causes dermatitis and respiratory disorders in human and dermatitis in cattle.
Stratification is the key character of the ecosystem and refers to the arrangement of flora and fauna in vertical layers.
Parenchyma with large intercellular spaces filled with air are termed as aerenchyma which serves to maintain buoyancy of aquatic plants in aquatic medium and allow them to float.
Opuntia is a xerophyte plant. Age pyramid represents distribution of organisms of a population in different age groups.
Therefore, the correct answer is option b.

Question: Alexander Von Humbolt described for the first time
Solution: Alexander Von Humbolt discovered species-area relationships. Species area relationships are the relationship between the area and the particular habitat. It is dependent on immigration, extinction, and clustering, etc. This relation can be graphically expressed as a rectangular hyperbola.

Question: Habitat loss and fragmentation, over exploitation, alien species invasion and co-extinction are causes for:
Solution: 'The Evil Quartet' of biodiversity loss is a concept that describes the reason that causes extinction of species. These main four reasons are overexploitation, loss of habitat, extinction of species continuously and introduction of the exotic species.

Question: Which of the following represents the maximum number of species among global biodiversity?
Solution: Fungi represent the maximum number of species among global biodiversity.

Question: Which of the following regions of the globe exhibits highest species diversity?
Solution: Species diversity is the number of different species that are present in a given community.  Amazon forest is the richest ecosystem on earth.  It contains millions of species of plants, insects, birds and other forms of life, many are still unrecorded by scientists.

Question: The type of conservation in which the threatened species are taken out from their natural habitat and placed in special setting when they can he protected and given special care is called
Solution: Ex situ Conservation – In this approach, threatened animals and plants are taken out from their natural habitat and placed in special setting where they can be protected and given special care.

Question:
Following are the statements with reference to 'lipids'.
(a) Lipids having only single bonds are called unsaturated fatty acids.
(b) Lecithin is a phospholipid
(c) Trihydroxy propane is glycerol.
(d) Palmitic acid has 20 carbon atoms including carboxyl carbon
(e) Arachidonic acid has 16 carbon atoms
Choose the correct answer from the options given below
Options: (b) and (c) only
(b) and (e) only
(a) and (b) only
(c) and (d) only
Solution: The correct option is (a) because lipids having only single bonds are called saturated fatty acids and lipids having one or more C = C double bonds are called unsaturated fatty acids. Palmitic acid has 16 carbon atoms including carboxyl carbon. Arachidonic acid has 20 carbon atoms including carboxyl carbon. Lecithin is a phospholipid found in the cell membrane. Glycerol has 3 carbons, each bearing a hydroxyl (–OH) group.

Question: Which of the following organisms are known as chief producers in the oceans?
Solution: Phytoplankton is the producer in the ocean. Diatoms belong to the class of phytoplankton which are known as chief producers in the ocean.

Question: Select the wrong statement.
Options: Pseudopodia are locomotory and feeding structures in Sporozoans
Cell wall is present in members of Fungi and Plantae
Mitochondria are the powerhouse of the cell in all kingdoms except monera
Solution:
Pseudopodia are the finger-like projections and are the locomotory organs of the protozoan protists. For example, Amoeba.
The cell wall is present in the members of both the kingdom Fungi and Plantae. Fungi have a cell wall made up of chitin and polysaccharides while plants' cell wall is made up of cellulose and hemicellulose.
Mitochondria are called the powerhouse of the cell because these are associated with cellular respiration. Mitochondria is present only in eukaryotes and absent in prokaryotes i.e. in members of kingdom Monera.
Mushrooms belong to the class Basidiomycetes of kingdom Fungi. For example, Agaricus.

Question: Identify the asexual reproductive structure associated with Penicillium:
Solution: In Penicillium, asexual spores called conidia produced exogenously on the special mycelium called conidiophores. Conidia, upon germination, produce mycelium.

Question: Thiobacillus is a group of bacteria helpful in carrying out
Solution: Thiobacillus denitrificans cause denitrification i.e., conversion of oxides of nitrogen to free N2.

Question: Floridean starch has structure similar to
Solution: Floridean starch is the storage substance found in red algae (Rhodophyta).  The structure of this compound is similar to amylopectin and glycogen.

Question: Pigment-containing membranous extensions in some cyanobacteria are
Solution: In cyanobacteria, chromatophores are the photosynthetic pigments containing membranous extensions. These pigments are mainly chlorophylls and impart their peculiar bluish green colour.

Question:
Match List-I with List-II.
List-IList-II
A. Fusion of protoplasms between gametesI. Meiosis
B. Fusion of two nucleiII. Plasmogamy
C. Generation of haploid sporesIII. Karyogamy

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
Solution: Karyogamy involve fusion of two nuclei. Plasmogamy is fusion of protoplasm. Meiosis leads to the production of haploid spores.

Question: After karyogamy followed by meiosis, spores are produced exogenously in
Solution:
In Agaricus (a genus of basidiomycetes), basidiospores are produced exogenously on a basidium.
Neurospora (a genus of ascomycetes) produces ascospores but endogenously inside the ascus.
Alternaria (a genus of Deuteromycetes) does not produce sexual spores.
Saccharomyces (Unicellular ascomycetes) produces ascospores endogenously.

Question: Which one of the following fungi contains hallucinogens?
Solution: Hallucinogen is the psychoactive agent that causes hallucination. It can be found in some plants and mushrooms. Amanita muscaria or fly amanita is a mushroom and psychoactive basidiomycete fungus, in the genus Amanita produces muscimol which has hallucinogenic properties.

Question:
Match the following
Column IColumn II
(a) Inhibitor of catalytic Activity(i) Ricin
(b) Possess peptide bonds(ii) Malonate
(c) Cell wall material in fungi(iii) Chitin
(d) Secondary metabolite(iv) Collagen
Options: a – iii; b – iv; c – i; d – ii
a – ii; b – iii; c – i; d – iv
a – ii; b – iv; c – iii; d – i
a – iii; b – i; c – iv; d – ii
Solution: Malonate resembles succinate in structure.  Succinate is the substrate of the enzyme succinate dehydrogenase.  Hence malonate acts as a competitive inhibitor of this  enzyme. Collagen is a protein, and hence contains peptide bonds. Cell wall of fungi is made of chitin. Ricin, a toxin, is a secondary metabolite.

Question: Which of the following is correct about viroids?
Options: They have DNA with a protein coat
They have free DNA without a protein coat
They have RNA with a protein coat
They have free RNA without a protein coat
Solution: Viroids have free RNA without a protein coat.  Potato spindle tuber disease is a disease caused by viroids.

Question: Select the WRONG statement.
Options: ‘Diatomaceous earth’ is formed by the cell walls of diatoms
Diatoms are chief producers in the oceans
Diatoms are microscopic and float passively in water
The walls of diatoms are easily destructible
Solution: Diatoms are unicellular eukaryotes placed under the kingdom Protista and serve as main producers in oceans. The cell of diatoms is surrounded by the cell wall, deposited with silica, called as frustules which are composed of two overlapping halves (the epitheca and the hypotheca).  Shells of diatoms are highly resistant to decomposition due to the presence of silica and are deposited in ocean beds leading to the formation of the diatomaceous earth. The gliding movement of floating diatoms is a passive process, facilitated by secretion of slimy material. Therefore, the correct answer is option d.

Question:
Each of the following characteristics represent a Kingdom proposed by Whittaker. Arrange the following in increasing order of complexity of body organization.
A. Multicellular heterotrophs with cell wall made of chitin.
B. Heterotrophs with tissue/organ/organ system level of body organization.
C. Prokaryotes with cell wall made of polysaccharides and amino acids.
D. Eukaryotic autotrophs with tissue/organ level of body organization.
E. Eukaryotes with cellular body organization.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
Solution:
According to the R.H. Whittaker (1969)
1. Monera - Prokaryotic cell which cell wall made up of Non-cellulosic (Polysaccharide + amino acid)
2. Protista - Eukaryotic with cellular body organisation
3. Fungi - Eukaryotic multicelluar heterotrophs with cell wall made of chitin
4. Plantae - Eukaryotic autotrophs with tissue/ organ level of organisation.
5. Animalia - Heterotrophs with Tissue/organ/ organ system level of organisation.

Question: Select the WRONG statement.
Options: Pili and fimbriae are mainly involved in motility of bacterial cells
Bacterial cell wall is made up of peptidoglycan
Solution:
Fimbriae and pili are hair-like appendages present on the bacterial cell wall similar to flagella. They are shorter than flagella and more in number. They are involved in the bacterial conjugation, attachment to the surface and motility. They are present in both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria but more prevalent in Gram-negative bacteria.
Fimbriae and pili are extensions of the cytoplasmic membrane and are made up of an oligomeric protein known as pilin.

Question: Pick out the wrong statement.
Options: Cell wall is absent in Animalia.
Protista have photosynthetic and heterotrophic modes of nutrition.
Nuclear membrane is present in Monera.
Solution: Kingdom Monera includes prokaryotes in which nuclear membrane is absent. Hence, the (d) statement is wrong.

Question: Which among the following is not a prokaryote?
Solution:
Saccharomyces i.e. yeast is a eukaryote (unicellular fungi).
Mycobacterium is a bacteria i.e. prokaryote.
Anabaena and Nostoc are cyanobacteria; they are also prokaryotes.

Question: An example of colonial alga is
Solution: Volvox is a motile colonial freshwater alga with a definite number of vegetative cells. It is a unicellular green alga found in freshwater bodies. It is photosynthetic in nature.

Question: Which of the following are the most suitable indicators of SO<sub>2</sub> pollution in the environment?
Solution: Lichens are useful bioindicators for air pollution, especially sulphur dioxide pollution since they derive essential nutrients from sulphur dioxide. If air is badly polluted with SO<sub>2,</sub> there may be no lichens present.

Question: Which of the following statements is correct?
Options: Organisms that depend on living plants are called saprophytes
Some of the organisms can fix atmospheric nitrogen in specialized cells called sheath cells
Fusion of two cells is called Karyogamy
Fusion of protoplasms between two motile or non-motile gametes is called plasmogamy
Solution:
Saprophytes are plants and animals which live on dead material and obtain all their nutrients. Some of these organisms can fix atmospheric nitrogen in specialized cells called heterocysts, e.g., Nostoc and Anabaena. The fusion of two nuclei is called karyogamy.

Hence, the (d) option is correct as fusion of protoplasms between two motile or non-motile gametes is called plasmogamy.
